source.title: How to attack Nighthaven Labs Basement (Tank/Armory)
source.notes: Single-site walkthrough for the Basement Tank/Armory area (same site as nighthaven-labs_defense.json's 'basement-tank'), planned and demonstrated using the community-made FPS Tactician tool rather than in-game footage — creator notes he could not find/re-record a live clip. Attack pushes from the top floor down through Rafters/IT, across the first floor Control room to open vertical sightlines ('vert') into Tank, then drops through the (currently unmodeled/'Michelle' placeholder, per creator) hatch above Animus/Tank to plant in the back corner, with Capitao flaming two doors from above and Snake holding Cargo Stairs. Auto-generated captions: 'Solid Snake' = Snake (the Metal Gear Solid collaboration operator native to this map — a genuine Attack-side pick, not a mishearing); 'Cade'/'Kaid claw' = Kaid, referenced only as a defender to consider banning, not an attack pick. Near the end the creator lists hard-breach substitutes for Grim/Capitao and says 'Demoman even' — this does not match any real Siege operator; likely ASR noise or an off-hand joke rather than an intended operator name, so it is not used as a recommendation below and is flagged here rather than silently mapped to a real operator. 'Zeno'/'zeze' = the room Nano (Nano Tech), corrected in callouts. Credit is given in-transcript to a player named George/GC.Official for originating this attack's core idea. No labeled, text-annotated official top-down blueprint could be found for this map at rewrite time, so no compass directions have been added to this file's locations — they remain room/feature names only, per the parsing spec.

sites.floor: Basement (attacked via Top Floor / First Floor)

hatches.location: Hatch above Animus/Tank corner (creator notes the map currently has no modeled hatch here — a placeholder square used in the planning tool)
hatches.note: Primary drop point for the plant. Opened once Buck has cut the vert into Tank and it's confirmed clear via drone. Nomad drops and plants tucked into the back corner directly below.
Hard-breach charge to open this hatch as part of establishing first-floor control; either operator can take the job.

hatches.location: Hatch at top of Cargo Stairs
hatches.note: Second hard-breach charge opened alongside the Warehouse hatch.

gadgetPlacements.note: Entry point into the map — drone Rafters and IT first, then hop in through this window and shoot the default cameras.

gadgetPlacements.location: Door from IT toward Server (not the first door, the next one along)
gadgetPlacements.note: Airjab Launcher on the second door, placed high on the door frame — harder for defenders to shoot out safely.

gadgetPlacements.location: Storage door (into the games lounge/vending area)
gadgetPlacements.note: Second Airjab Launcher on this door, not the closer one.
Third Airjab Launcher, covering the Cargo approach.

gadgetPlacements.location: Control room — three lines of sight into Tank
gadgetPlacements.note: Shotgun three lines of sight through the Control floor: one long line aligned with the window, one adjacent to one wall (offset slightly in), one down the middle. Denies anywhere safe to hide in Tank except directly behind the tank object itself.

gadgetPlacements.location: Two door frames above Tank (CCTV side)
gadgetPlacements.note: Use Buck's vert holes to drop fire onto the two doors defenders use to contest the plant corner.

Use scanner pre-round to confirm Fish Stairs, back CCTV, and back Cargo Stairs are clear for Nomad's entry, then hold Cargo Stairs for most of the round as a deterrent. Watch for a Nano-door line of sight onto the stairs. Optionally bring a breaching charge to open extra vert here instead of just holding an angle.

gadgetPlacements.location: Breach wall behind the plant corner
gadgetPlacements.note: After the plant is down, drop the hatch above, clear a Bandit battery or Kaid claw off this wall, and open it for a safer outside position to play post-plant/deny the defuse. Only viable if this wall isn't reinforced.

operatorRecommendations.note: Opens a Warehouse/Cargo Stairs hatch, then fires down through Buck's Control-room vert holes onto the two doors defenders use to contest the plant. In a minimal 3-stack, sprint to Cargo Stairs after flaming to help hold that flank.

Enters via the Rafters window, places three Airjab Launchers (IT-to-Server door, Storage door, Cargo door), then drops the basement hatch and plants in the back corner. Substitute Gridlock for the same air-jab job.

Gets into Control and shotguns three lines of sight down into Tank, denying nearly all hiding spots below. Can hold the doors from above post-plant if Snake needs backup. Sledge or Ram are substitutes for the same vert.

Uses his scanning gadget to pre-clear the entry route for Nomad, then holds/annoys at Cargo Stairs to stop the plant being denied from that side. Deimos or another frag-utility operator is a substitute if Snake is banned or unwanted.

Helps open the Warehouse/Cargo Stairs hatch, drones teammates in, and can open extra vert or a post-plant escape route with a hard-breach charge. Ying or Striker are substitutes for the hard-breach job.

generalTips.note: Minimum viable is 3 players — Capitao, Buck, and Nomad. Snake and Grim are optional additions. With only 3, Capitao has to sprint to Cargo Stairs after flaming the doors since there's no 5th to hold that flank.
Core premise: make vert into Tank from Control above (Buck), flame the two contested doors from that vert (Capitao), plant in the back corner of the hatch drop (Nomad).
Ban Kaid pre-round to guarantee the hatches/walls in this plan open cleanly, instead of bringing EMPs to counter a Kaid claw or electrified hatch.
Drone/re-check the Tank area immediately before dropping the hatch to plant — watch for a flanking defender or someone sitting motionless in a hiding spot until the plant starts.

Post-plant, keep Snake alive and active on Cargo Stairs — it denies the only other flank route into the plant corner (aside from the air-jabbed Storage/Cargo doors above) during a defender retake push.
